name: Cloquet Transit Co
districts_or_customers: Cloquet Public Schools (ISD 94) — 74-year contract; will end Aug 1, 2026 (lost to Palmer Bus Service)
recent_news: April 2026: Cloquet Public Schools announced transition from Cloquet Transit to Palmer Bus Service effective Aug 1, 2026, after 74 years; company stated school bus segment had struggled to remain profitable and will refocus on coach buses via LCS Coaches.
fit_notes: Diminished tuck-in case: company is actively exiting school bus operations and lost its anchor 74-year district contract effective Aug 2026. Possible asset/route acquisition only — limited going-concern value as a school bus platform. Family principals (Jeff/Jack Lane) at natural transition point.

name: Lincoln Coach Lines
districts_or_customers: Hanover Public School District (contracted home-to-school transportation)
recent_news: No M&A news found. Continues to operate dual school-bus and charter/tour business in Hanover/York County PA region; still Lightner-family owned per company materials.
fit_notes: Attractive small PA tuck-in: 78-year-old family business with anchor Hanover district contract and a charter/tour overlay. High succession signal (2nd/3rd-gen Lightner family). Geographically complementary to Summit's PA footprint; small but stable contract base.

name: Leon George II School Buses
recent_news: No recent M&A news. Operates two PA locations (Palmerton + Lehighton, the second added 1995). Business has shifted from charter-focused to predominantly school routes per family history page.
fit_notes: Classic small-PA family tuck-in: 2nd-gen owner Leon II since 1989, 3-4 contiguous Carbon/Lehigh county district contracts, multi-location footprint. Succession signal medium (owner likely 50s-60s, no clear 3rd-gen successor mentioned). Good geographic fit for Summit's PA expansion.

name: Fullington (Bellefonte branch)
principals: Jennifer Corman (Director of Sales & Customer Experience)
districts_or_customers: Centre County area school districts via parent Fullington network (specific district list not disclosed)
recent_news: Fullington parent partners with Megabus for scheduled service from State College serving 18 PA cities including Bellefonte. No M&A activity announced for the Bellefonte branch specifically.
fit_notes: This is the Bellefonte satellite of 100+ year old Fullington Auto Bus (already covered by rank #1 Hollidaysburg HQ). Not an independent target — any deal would be at parent company level (11 PA locations, school + motorcoach + Trailways line-haul). High strategic interest as a platform-scale PA acquisition rather than tuck-in; succession unclear but multi-generational Fullington family business.

name: Smyth Buss Co
districts_or_customers: Suffield VOAG and 8 additional out-of-town programs
recent_news: Bob Smyth acquired the company from estate of late brother Dick in 2017 following Dick's unexpected 2015 death. Recognized by School Bus Fleet as a 'Great Fleet'. No subsequent M&A news; record lists NY but operations are physically in Enfield, CT.
fit_notes: Data file misidentifies state — company is in Enfield, CT (NY listing appears to be a billing or corporate-entity address). 150-bus fleet across three adjacent CT districts is meaningful tuck-in scale. High succession signal: current owner Bob Smyth acquired from late brother's estate at age likely 60+; no obvious next-gen successor mentioned in family materials.
